summary:
- File concerns the **Indian Immigration Fund (Malaya)** during **WWII/Japanese occupation**, focusing on **safeguarding and administering the Fund’s assets outside Malaya** and maintaining continuity for post-war labour/immigration planning (main correspondence **May–Nov 1942**; file marked **1942–1946**, “closed until 1972”).  
- Background notes trace the Fund’s origins to an **enactment dating to 1907**, empowering an enquiry/immigration body to levy assessments on employers of labour from the **Madras Presidency**; the controlling **Indian Immigration/Enquiry Committee** is described as a **statutory corporate body** appointed by the **Governor in Council** (references to **Eastern Code, Cap. 69 Part 14; Sec. 113**).  
- Key issue raised: **who is responsible for Fund assets held outside Malaya** (some securities apparently in **London**, and **property in India**—e.g., **Avadi or Malpakkam** mentioned, with an agreement involving the **Government of India/Indian Defence Department**; details partly unclear due to OCR).  
- The **Rubber Growers’ Association (RGA)** (and referenced **United Planting Association of Malaya**) presses the **Colonial Office/Secretary of State** to protect the Fund (derived largely from plantation estates) and suggests a **“skeleton organisation”** or designated official/committee to keep in touch with labour-supply issues and potentially liaise with **India** and the **Netherlands East Indies**.  
- The **Secretary of State (Viscount Cranborne/Granborne)** replies (drafts/letters dated **May–June 1942**) that it is **not practicable to reconstitute the Indian Immigration Committee** during occupation; instead he would **assume responsibility “as far as practicable” for assets outside Malaya**, similar to arrangements for the **Rubber Fund** and **Rubber Research Institute of Malaya**.  
- Notable assets/numbers: Indian Immigration Fund holdings listed as **£8,000 Tanganyika Guaranteed 4% (1951–71)**, **£10,000 Nyasaland 3% (1954–74)**, and **£207,300 3½% War Loan**, held via the **Chartered Bank of India, Australia & China** and registered in **St. Helens Nominees Ltd**; Rubber Research Institute noted as holding **£3,000 British 3½% War Loan** (also via the Chartered Bank/St Helens Nominees).  
- Operational arrangements: the **Acting Emigration Commissioner for Malaya in India** is said to have continued responsibility for the Fund’s **Madras** property/assets under pre-occupation instructions, subject to future direction; the Colonial Office notes it **cannot assume responsibility for cash/assets in Malaya** and that **interest payments on certain Malayan local/municipal loans** are restricted in wartime conditions.
